stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Some atoms have a very large nucleus.
  • The nucleus contains protons and neutrons bound together.
  • An atom of uranium-238 has a particularly massive nucleus.
  • The diameter of its nucleus is approximately 15 femtometers.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the diameter of uranium-238's nucleus. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: An atom of uranium-238, like some other atoms, has a massive nucleus.
The nucleus of an atom of uranium-238 has a diameter of approximately 15 femtometers.
Atoms with massive nuclei contain many protons and neutrons.
Protons and neutrons are bound together at the surface of an atom of uranium-238.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the diameter of uranium-238's nucleus, noting that it is approximately 15 femtometers.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence states that uranium-238 has a massive nucleus; it doesn't emphasize the diameter of that nucleus. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence makes a generalization about atoms; it doesn't emphasize the diameter of uranium-238's nucleus. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes the location of protons and neutrons; it doesn't emphasize the diameter of uranium-238's nucleus.
